cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) was concerned about when the jade ring design was proposed. The options are A through D. First, I'll need to look through the provided text to find the relevant information. The passage mentions that experts from the IOC were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. It also states that the IOC required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test, and the Shanghai Mint even raised that to 3 meters. Medals could survive a 3-meter drop with only minor cracks on the jade rings. So the main concern here is about the jade rings breaking or being fragile. Let's look at the options again: A) the medals will be worth less – The passage doesn't mention anything about the monetary value of the medals, so this seems unlikely. B) the medals are too difficult to make – While the text does mention that the technology is not easy and involves a painstaking manual process, the IOC's specific concern was about fragility, not the difficulty of making them. C) competitors don't like the design – There's no mention of competitors' opinions in the text, so this option doesn't seem correct. D) jade
